title: The C Word

jokes.text: Yo, is this about that little red-headed intern? 'Cause she asked me to take it out.
jokes.setup: Someone being called in to speak to a boss, suggesting potential trouble
jokes.punchline: Misinterpretation that sounds sexual but reveals innocent context later
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection that pays off later. Character-appropriate misunderstanding.

jokes.text: I'm not familiar with about half the words in that sentence.
jokes.setup: Jack using fancy corporate speak about charity golf tournament
jokes.punchline: Tracy's honest admission of not understanding the jargon
jokes.explanation: Perfect character beat - Tracy's outsider status to corporate world

jokes.text: Is that the gay guy from Project Runway?
jokes.setup: Jack mentioning Don Geiss, the powerful CEO
jokes.punchline: Tracy conflating him with reality TV personality
jokes.explanation: Good character consistency - Tracy's pop culture confusion

jokes.text: The dude from my checks?
jokes.setup: Jack explaining Geiss is the CEO
jokes.punchline: Tracy's practical way of understanding corporate hierarchy
jokes.explanation: Excellent economy and character voice - reduces powerful CEO to paycheck signer

jokes.text: Okay false alarm. It turns out she asked him to take it out.
jokes.setup: The earlier sexual misunderstanding about the intern
jokes.punchline: Reveals innocent context - trash removal
jokes.explanation: Good callback that resolves the misdirection properly

jokes.text: I'll see you in CT, Jackie D.
jokes.setup: Normal farewell
jokes.punchline: Tracy giving Jack a street nickname
jokes.explanation: Character-appropriate nickname giving

jokes.text: Was Courtney Love not available?
jokes.setup: Jack explaining his choice of Tracy as golf partner
jokes.punchline: Comparing Tracy to notoriously unpredictable celebrity
jokes.explanation: Sharp comparison highlighting Tracy's unpredictability

jokes.text: You might want to rephrase that.
jokes.setup: Jack saying 'being in a foursome with this man can change your life'
jokes.punchline: Liz noting the sexual double entendre
jokes.explanation: Standard innuendo catch but well-executed

jokes.text: I painted it myself.
jokes.setup: Liz asking about a portrait (presumably of Geiss)
jokes.punchline: Jack admits to painting it himself, revealing obsession
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack character moment - his corporate worship taken to absurd lengths

jokes.text: She always sounds so terrified. She's always, like, The President has a plan for Iran, and we are currently adhering to that plan.
jokes.setup: Discussing Condoleezza Rice on Meet the Press
jokes.punchline: Impersonation highlighting nervous political speak
jokes.explanation: Good political observation with specific impersonation

jokes.text: I stayed up all night watching a Designing Women marathon.
jokes.setup: Frank being exhausted and unable to focus
jokes.punchline: Revealing he was watching stereotypically feminine TV
jokes.explanation: Good character subversion - tough guy watching Designing Women

jokes.text: At first, I hated it. And then I liked it. And then I hated it again. And then I got horny, and then I fell asleep.
jokes.setup: Frank's confession about watching Designing Women
jokes.punchline: Toofer's detailed emotional journey including inappropriate arousal
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation with perfect timing and unexpected honesty

jokes.text: Ay, pero ay Dios mio!
jokes.setup: Liz telling Lutz not to eat while working
jokes.punchline: Lutz's dramatic Spanish exclamation (presumably while eating)
jokes.explanation: Standard physical comedy beat

jokes.text: I didn't like it two weeks ago when it was called America's Next Top Hobo, and I didn't like it a month ago, when it was called Hobo Eye for the Straight Guy.
jokes.setup: Writer pitching Dancing with the Hobo sketch
jokes.punchline: Revealing this is the same bad idea with different reality show names
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation showing the writer's persistence with bad ideas

jokes.text: Deal or No Hobo?
jokes.setup: The series of hobo-themed reality show parodies
jokes.punchline: Another variation continuing the pattern
jokes.explanation: Good continuation of the escalating pattern

jokes.text: Hey, Lutz, why don't you approach your job with the same creativity and excitement you have for all-you-can-eat buffets?
jokes.setup: Lutz suggesting another bad hobo idea
jokes.punchline: Liz comparing his work ethic to his eating habits
jokes.explanation: Sharp character-specific insult with good structure

jokes.text: We have a past. A romantic past.
jokes.setup: Kenneth saying he can't work with Grace
jokes.punchline: The innocent page having a 'romantic past'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kenneth character moment - his earnestness about romance

jokes.text: Well, Grace, it's been a pleasure talking to you.
jokes.setup: The revelation about their romantic past
jokes.punchline: Kenneth immediately trying to end the conversation formally
jokes.explanation: Great Kenneth character beat - his awkward formality

jokes.text: Oh, Kenneth, if you're worried about disgracing the National Broadcasting Company, you're too late.
jokes.setup: Kenneth worried about disgracing 'the peacock' (NBC)
jokes.punchline: Grace pointing out NBC's already damaged reputation
jokes.explanation: Excellent meta joke about the network airing the show

jokes.text: Doggone it, Grace, I just don't know myself around you.
jokes.setup: Kenneth trying to be professional with Grace
jokes.punchline: His old-fashioned romantic confession
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kenneth voice - earnest and old-fashioned

jokes.text: Like-- That won't be necessary. I'll probably just do it anyway.
jokes.setup: Offering to signal when Don Geiss arrives
jokes.punchline: Jack cutting him off but the employee persisting anyway
jokes.explanation: Good awkward service employee moment

jokes.text: They're very good at sensing debilitating loneliness in a person.
jokes.setup: Talking about animals liking Liz
jokes.punchline: Dark assessment of why animals are drawn to her
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark self-awareness from Liz

jokes.text: I'm allergic to anything warm and adorable.
jokes.setup: Suggestion that Liz adopt an animal
jokes.punchline: Her claiming to be allergic to positive traits
jokes.explanation: Great Liz character moment - her aversion to sentiment

jokes.text: Runt! What? This kitten, he's such a runt! I'm gonna name you Runty. You're such a little raging little runt!
jokes.setup: Someone calling Liz the C-word (seemingly)
jokes.punchline: She's actually talking to a kitten named Runt
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection that sets up the episode's central misunderstanding

jokes.text: No, I don't want to get in it. I want to blow it up and run away from it in slow motion.
jokes.setup: Being offered a helicopter ride
jokes.punchline: Tracy wanting to use it for action movie sequence
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tracy Jordan movie star logic

jokes.text: The one the rhymes with the name of your favorite Todd Rundgren album.
jokes.setup: Trying to identify what word Lutz called her
jokes.punchline: Obscure musical reference instead of saying the word
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly nerdy Liz way to avoid saying a curse word

jokes.text: It rhymes with Hermit of Mink Hollow?
jokes.setup: Liz's Todd Rundgren reference
jokes.punchline: Frank knowing a different Rundgren album
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Frank's unexpected music knowledge

jokes.text: There is no male equivalent to this word.
jokes.setup: Explaining why the C-word is particularly offensive
jokes.punchline: Pointing out the gendered nature of insults
jokes.explanation: Smart social observation that drives the plot

jokes.text: Like...monkus. Fungdark.
jokes.setup: Trying to create a male equivalent to the C-word
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous made-up words
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist made-up words

jokes.text: Don't tell me to calm down you fungdark.
jokes.setup: The made-up insult 'fungdark'
jokes.punchline: Liz immediately using it in anger
jokes.explanation: Good immediate callback with character consistency

jokes.text: It doesn't work.
jokes.setup: Using the made-up insult 'fungdark'
jokes.punchline: Immediately recognizing it's not satisfying
jokes.explanation: Good self-aware moment

jokes.text: Lutz has been totally slacking off since his grandma died.
jokes.setup: Discussing firing Lutz
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of tragic personal loss
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about grief affecting work performance

jokes.text: I thought you knew and you were just being a... You know, that word Lutz called you.
jokes.setup: Explaining why they didn't tell Liz about the grandmother
jokes.punchline: Suggesting Liz was being the C-word by not acknowledging it
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the central conflict

jokes.text: Hey, look everybody Sherlock Homo is here to solve the case of the gay sweater.
jokes.setup: Someone entering wearing what's presumably a flamboyant sweater
jokes.punchline: Frank's juvenile homophobic wordplay
jokes.explanation: Typical Frank juvenile humor, character-appropriate but not clever

jokes.text: Oh, my God, I am. I'm a total... Runt! I lost my kitten. Has anyone seen my runt?
jokes.setup: Liz realizing she might be acting like the C-word
jokes.punchline: Another misdirection with the kitten name
jokes.explanation: Good callback to the misdirection setup

jokes.text: Tomorrow I'm gonna be in an intense six hour foursome with three other men. And one of them will be Don Geiss and he's gonna get all of my attention. And you're just gonna sit back and watch.
jokes.setup: Jack describing the golf outing
jokes.punchline: Unintentional sexual double entendre
jokes.explanation: Standard innuendo but well-executed with Jack's obliviousness

jokes.text: You know the army's been messing with the sun. That's why I keep my junk covered. Once that stuff get in your hang, you're done.
jokes.setup: Casual conversation
jokes.punchline: Tracy's conspiracy theory about solar radiation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tracy conspiracy theory with anatomical concerns

jokes.text: This guys spends so much time in the sand that his nickname should be Falu-ser.
jokes.setup: Jack bragging about Tracy's golf game to Geiss
jokes.punchline: Terrible golf pun (Faldo + loser)
jokes.explanation: Appropriately bad Jack attempt at golf humor

jokes.text: Hey, how come you don't hire more black people around here?
jokes.setup: Polite corporate gathering
jokes.punchline: Tracy's blunt question about diversity
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tracy moment - disrupting polite corporate facade

jokes.text: How come there's just me and Carlton over there?
jokes.setup: Tracy questioning lack of diversity
jokes.punchline: Referring to the only other black person as Carlton Banks
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with character-appropriate pop culture reference

jokes.text: I believe Tracy's referring to Carlton, the straight laced brother from Fresh Prince of Bel Air, 1990 through 1996. On NBC.
jokes.setup: Tracy's Carlton reference
jokes.punchline: Kenneth's overly detailed explanation including network reference
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kenneth moment with meta NBC reference

jokes.text: I'm supposed to just be a funny black man who says funny things.
jokes.setup: Jack criticizing Tracy's behavior
jokes.punchline: Tracy calling out his role as token entertainment
jokes.explanation: Sharp meta-commentary on racial tokenism

jokes.text: Bartender, could you bring me a Mustang Melon and a bag of barbeque potato chips? 'Cause apparently I'm only here to be a stereotype.
jokes.setup: Tracy's complaint about being stereotyped
jokes.punchline: Immediately ordering stereotypical items
jokes.explanation: Excellent ironic escalation - embodying stereotype while protesting it

jokes.text: Son, I wasn't joking about those chips.
jokes.setup: The bartender presumably not bringing chips
jokes.punchline: Tracy seriously wanting them despite the protest context
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Tracy's genuine desire for the chips

jokes.text: No, it's two dozen cupcakes with melted mini-candy bars in the middle.
jokes.setup: Frank thinking Liz made brisket
jokes.punchline: Describing an over-the-top dessert creation
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Liz's overcompensation

jokes.text: I'm a nice person you bald, gangly...
jokes.setup: Liz trying to prove she's nice
jokes.punchline: Immediately starting to insult Frank
jokes.explanation: Perfect contradiction of her stated intent

jokes.text: I studied TV theory at Kentucky Mountain Bible College.
jokes.setup: Kenneth explaining his qualifications
jokes.punchline: Combining television and fundamentalist education
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kenneth backstory detail - absurd but believable for character

jokes.text: I'm ridiculous. I'm black! I may even be ugly. But dear God... I'm here! I'm here! And nothing but them can keep me from it!
jokes.setup: Tracy's performative rebellion
jokes.punchline: Over-the-top dramatic declaration
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Tracy's dramatic performance

jokes.text: This isn't Hitler's bunker. That would make me Hitler.
jokes.setup: Someone apologizing for taking a phone call
jokes.punchline: Jack's inappropriate historical comparison
jokes.explanation: Good Jack moment - inappropriate but logical comparison

jokes.text: Before you answer, superballs!
jokes.setup: Liz asking if they like the new her
jokes.punchline: Throwing superballs to demonstrate fun personality
jokes.explanation: Good physical gag showing Liz's awkward attempt at fun

jokes.text: God, it's like dating Katie Couric all over again.
jokes.setup: Tracy complaining about being used
jokes.punchline: Comparing it to dating famous news anchor
jokes.explanation: Good Tracy celebrity name-drop with implied backstory

jokes.text: Ted, who's best known for getting caught using a corporate credit card at a gay strip club.
jokes.setup: Jack explaining who they're playing golf with instead
jokes.punchline: Casually revealing Ted's scandal
jokes.explanation: Good casual reveal of corporate scandal

jokes.text: I'm sorry, Amanda, you were bound to find that out eventually.
jokes.setup: Revealing Ted's gay strip club scandal
jokes.punchline: Addressing Ted's wife who's apparently present
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation making the scandal revelation worse

jokes.text: If I thought I left my coffeemaker on, I wouldn't be able to focus either. No, no, I get it. Everyone should see Mama Mia before it closes.
jokes.setup: Writers all leaving with personal problems
jokes.punchline: Liz making increasingly obvious excuses for them
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Liz's denial about staff abandoning her

jokes.text: Oh, hey, could I borrow 60 bucks? I'm going to meet Frank and Lutz at Scores.
jokes.setup: Toofer giving advice about standing up for yourself
jokes.punchline: Immediately asking to borrow money for strip club
jokes.explanation: Excellent undercut of his advice with immediate hypocrisy

jokes.text: When my daughter Shahita was born, she had chubby arms and legs and a big bald head. She looked like my Uncle Rupert in a diaper.
jokes.setup: Tracy beginning heartfelt speech about diabetes
jokes.punchline: Unflattering comparison of baby to uncle
jokes.explanation: Good character-appropriate humor within serious speech

jokes.text: We stayed up all night the night before watching bible movies and eating Fiddle Faddle.
jokes.setup: Tracy's story about Easter with his daughter
jokes.punchline: Specific details that sound authentically Tracy
jokes.explanation: Good specific details that feel authentic to Tracy's character

jokes.text: She threw up right in the middle of the Jaguar dealership.
jokes.setup: Story about daughter getting sick on Easter
jokes.punchline: Specific location that reveals Tracy's lifestyle
jokes.explanation: Good detail that casually reveals wealth while maintaining story

jokes.text: I don't have a daughter.
jokes.setup: Tracy's entire heartfelt speech about his daughter's diabetes
jokes.punchline: Revealing he made it all up
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection - completely recontextualizes the entire speech. Maximum economy and impact.

jokes.text: So to quote Mr. Jerry McGuire....you make me a complete person.
jokes.setup: Kenneth making romantic declaration to Grace
jokes.punchline: Quoting Jerry Maguire incorrectly
jokes.explanation: Good Kenneth character moment - earnest but slightly wrong

jokes.text: When you said hello, you had me.
jokes.setup: Kenneth's Jerry Maguire quotation
jokes.punchline: Another slightly incorrect movie quote
jokes.explanation: Good continuation of Kenneth's earnest misquoting

jokes.text: Sir, you may be right. I may be not capable of understanding normal thinking because I think a woman is just as powerful as a man. And until you learn to respect me, you better learn to stay out of my way. 'Cause this is my house.
jokes.setup: The Designing Women marathon that's been referenced
jokes.punchline: The exact empowering speech Liz wants to use
jokes.explanation: Good callback that sets up Liz's later speech

jokes.text: Staying up all night re-writing The Amazing Hobo. You fat nerd.
jokes.setup: Liz working alone
jokes.punchline: Callback to hobo sketches plus self-insult
jokes.explanation: Good callback with character self-awareness

jokes.text: This is the men's room. Oh!
jokes.setup: Liz looking exhausted
jokes.punchline: She's accidentally in the wrong bathroom
jokes.explanation: Standard physical comedy showing Liz's exhaustion

jokes.text: Next weekend Tracy and I are gonna double team Don Geiss with our big ideas.
jokes.setup: Jack explaining his success
jokes.punchline: Another unintentional sexual double entendre
jokes.explanation: Standard innuendo joke but consistent with Jack's obliviousness

jokes.text: Because I'm not just feminine, I also can project my power.
jokes.setup: Liz preparing to yell at her staff
jokes.punchline: Her awkward attempt at feminist empowerment
jokes.explanation: Good Liz character moment showing her awkward empowerment

jokes.text: That's not cool.
jokes.setup: Writer complaining about promised snickerdoodles
jokes.punchline: Treating it as a serious betrayal
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan treatment of trivial complaint

jokes.text: What is aux?
jokes.setup: Liz trying to operate TV/VCR
jokes.punchline: Her technological incompetence ruining dramatic moment
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment undermining Liz's attempt at authority

jokes.text: Please don't make me move back to Alaska, Liz. I hate it there.
jokes.setup: Liz threatening to fire Lutz
jokes.punchline: His specific fear about returning to Alaska
jokes.explanation: Good character detail and escalation of consequences

jokes.text: And you will never alter drapes in Atlanta again. Because you do not cross a Sugarbaker woman!
jokes.setup: Liz's threat to the staff
jokes.punchline: Mixing her real threat with Designing Women dialogue
jokes.explanation: Excellent payoff to the Designing Women setup with perfect character confusion

jokes.text: It's nonny time.
jokes.setup: Jack comforting exhausted Liz
jokes.punchline: His weird term for nap time
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack character moment - caring but weird. Great economy.

tmdb_overview: As shocked as she is to be called the c-word by Lutz, Liz is more horrified to realize he may be right. Meanwhile, Jack brings Tracy to a charity golf event to get closer to the chairman, however his plan backfires when Tracy acts too much like his uncensored self.
insight: 71 jokes in 22 minutes—high-volume approach, but impact lags density.
